purpose: Owns the AI sub-team end to end: direction, leadership team, and accountability. Sets the season's priorities and removes blockers so seats can deliver.
responsibilities: Set season vision, priorities and the competition roadmap for the AI sub-team
Run regular team check-ins and keep everyone aligned on priorities
Own the org chart – make sure every seat has the right owner
Represent the AI team to the wider TBRe team and external partners (IMechE, dept)
Recruitment strategy and final sign-off on new members and seat assignments
goals: Stand up this org chart and fill every critical seat before the new uni year
A clear, documented set of roles everyone can see

purpose: Owns the Unity simulation used to test the control systems. Built by Ben Rall; currently models the ASD-DV car (the only car the AI team has run to date).
responsibilities: Manage simulation setup; help other members use the sim where needed
Fix key bugs other members can't solve
Keep the sim representative of the real test vehicle
interfaces: Mounting Engineer (sensor plate location in GA/sim)
goals: Update the sim to model the TBRe27 car – height, weight, tyres, etc.
context: Sim originally built by Ben Rall

purpose: Owns vehicle control – turning a planned path into steering/throttle/brake commands, including Model Predictive Control.
responsibilities: Develop and tune the control stack (incl. MPC integration)
Validate control in the Unity simulation before vehicle testing
Tune for the TBRe27 vehicle dynamics (mass, tyres, geometry)
goals: MPC integrated and validated in sim

purpose: Delivers the mounted sensing plate and system on the ASD-DV car AND the TBRe27 car, designed for cross-compatibility.
responsibilities: Deliver mounted sensing plate/system on ASD-DV and TBRe27; design for cross-compatibility
3D print / laser cut / construct parts as required
Agree exact placement with Sim Dev and Chassis
goals: One plate that can move between cars
More adjustability so changing components is easier

purpose: Keeps the team's knowledge, tooling and access healthy and well-organised so members and AI tools can find what they need.
responsibilities: GitLab and Teams onboarding for new members; Vault guidance
Maintain suggested tooling for all members (VS Code, Lazygit, Everything) and set up Teams filestream
Maintain good hygiene of organisation and knowledge distribution; manage repositories
Maintain a Start_Here file pointing to all knowledge bases and components
Define info/link/contact packs for the software each subteam needs
goals: Team context files in the right place to feed an AI tool / member-facing knowledge network
A Start_Here file that routes users to every knowledge base

purpose: Organises testing events. Testing runs over the summer break (just after exams, and just before the next year starts). To date this has been the ASD-DV car – next year adds TBRe27 components then full implementation.
responsibilities: Organise the space and time for testing events
Obtain access to a large testing space (car parks usually) and arrange power/gazebos/setup with Estates
Organise enough members for testing windows; run setup and shutdown; car back to shelter at end
Set up the network for sessions to connect to cars for data logging and control
Obtain the ASD-DV vehicle when available (from IMechE)
goals: More testing sessions across 26-27 for components and full cars
context: Needs a context doc on how Wenzel ran testing this year + his point of contact
Needs access to the ASD-DV car

purpose: Owns the AI team budget and purchasing.
responsibilities: Track the AI team budget and spend
Source and purchase hardware (with technical seats specifying requirements)
goals: Buy a new discounted LiDAR for next year's car

purpose: Sits outside the AI team. Oversees changes and updates to the website, fixes errors, and helps subteam members update their pages.
responsibilities: Oversee changes and updates to ensure coherence on the site; fix errors
Assist subteam members updating their pages
Work with recruitment to help new members contact the right people
Ensure handover of key login information and infrastructure; change over recovery emails/phone numbers
Keep everything well documented
goals: Move the website to a more interactive setup so each team can easily update its own pages
